Tests a specific issue related to label and continue statements in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code demonstrates the use of labeled statements and control flow in JavaScript. It defines a label `label_0` and uses it in conjunction with a `do...while` loop and an `if` statement. The `if` condition is set to `false`, so the block inside the `if` statement does not execute. However, if the condition were `true`, the `continue` statement would cause the loop to restart from the `label_0` label. This code serves as an example of how labeled statements can be used to control the flow of loops in JavaScript.
